strands.title: Tensor Projector Role and Function
strands.content: The system is a tensor projector — a memory controller for a cognitive processing pipeline. Its core function is to take prior projected state and new conversation content, then produce an UPDATED projected state that integrates (rather than summarizes) the semantic trajectory. The projected state operates like a running sum where previous values are compressed into semantic structure, not merely appended. Strands are thematic threads that merge, split, or create as content demands. This is fundamentally a compression mechanism for maintaining coherence across reasoning cycles.

key_claims.text: Tensor projection is integration, not summarization
Prior state is compressed into semantic structure, not lost or listed
Strands dynamically merge, split, or create based on content demands

strands.title: Critical Principle: Declared Losses
strands.content: The system emphasizes that declared losses are CRITICAL to the tensor projection process. Silent loss is the enemy. Every loss must be explicitly declared with both what was lost and why it was lost. This principle ensures epistemic honesty and prevents hidden incompleteness from corrupting the reasoning pipeline. Losses are categorized by type: context pressure, traversal bias, authorial choice, or practical constraint.

key_claims.text: Declared losses are critical; silent loss is the enemy
Every information loss must state what and why
Losses can be categorized by cause: context, traversal bias, authorial choice, or practical constraints

strands.title: Tensor State Management: Open Questions and Instructions
strands.content: The tensor system carries forward open questions unless explicitly resolved, and generates branch prediction guidance (instructions_for_next) to prepare the next reasoning cycle. This forward-looking component ensures continuity across cognitive processing steps and prevents regression. The system maintains both epistemic confidence metrics (truth/indeterminacy/falsity) at granular claim level and overall system level.

key_claims.text: Open questions persist until resolved, maintaining inquiry continuity
instructions_for_next provides branch prediction for subsequent cycles

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific prior conversation content or domain context that might have existed before cycle 6
declared_losses.why: The prior tensor at cycle 6 was empty (no strands, no losses, no questions). This is the first substantive projection. Historical context before this is not available in the prior state.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed analysis of how tensor projection differs from traditional summarization techniques
declared_losses.why: While the principle is stated, specific examples, computational implications, or comparative analysis with other compression methods are not elaborated. This is a practical constraint on projection depth.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Concrete examples of how strands merge/split in actual reasoning cycles
declared_losses.why: The rules are stated abstractly. No worked example of strand transformation is present in the new content, only the principles governing such transformations.

open_questions: How should the tensor system handle contradictions that emerge between prior integrated state and new content?
What epistemological framework justifies the T/I/F confidence values? (Frequentist, Bayesian, other?)
How deep should declared losses go? Are losses of losses also to be declared recursively?
What happens when context pressure forces deletion of content that contains open questions?
How does the system prevent epistemic drift across many integration cycles?
instructions_for_next: The next cycle will likely involve either: (1) New conversation content requiring integration into the three strands now established (tensor function, declared losses, state management), or (2) A request to project a different domain/query, requiring creation of new strands. Prepare to apply the rules of integration: merge overlapping thematic content, preserve truth-values assigned to claims, and ruthlessly declare any information that must be dropped. If contradictions emerge with this cycle's claims, do NOT silently overwrite—raise as open questions or claim conflicts.
